framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.what_it_is: Investigate why a structure failed - reconstruct loads, test materials, model the collapse mechanism - and publish the findings as a case study other engineers learn from.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can diagnose real structural behavior under uncertainty and turn hard lessons into durable, teachable knowledge, not just design to code.

paths.what_it_is: Lead the structural engineering of a record-setting or architecturally singular building or bridge, from concept through construction.
paths.why_it_signals: Delivering a landmark under real physical and safety constraints proves command of structural theory, materials, and construction pushed to the edge of what has been built before.

paths.what_it_is: Explain how structures work, and why they sometimes fail, to a broad public audience through video, writing, or talks - grounded in real practicing experience.
paths.why_it_signals: Translating rigorous engineering judgment into content a lay audience trusts shows a depth of understanding most practitioners never have to prove.
